item: xchacha-seal-rc: propagate rc through entire XChaCha20-Poly1305 public seal/init/aad path
reachability_fact: xchacha20_poly1305.iii line 37: `aead_chacha20_poly1305_init(&XC_SUBKEY as u64, &XC_NONCE as u64)` return is silently discarded. Line 38: `xc_setup` then returns `0i32` unconditionally. Line 42: `xchacha20_poly1305_seal` discards `xc_setup(...)`. Line 43: discards `aead_chacha20_poly1305_aad(...)`. Line 44: discards `aead_chacha20_poly1305_seal(...)`. Line 45: returns `0i32` (success) no matter what. Same pattern at lines 49-50 in `xchacha20_poly1305_open`. The one external caller (corpus/206_xchacha20_poly1305.iii line 51) calls `xchacha20_poly1305_seal(...)` with no binding, discarding the rc entirely. `aead_chacha20_poly1305_init` (chacha20_poly1305.iii line 88) returns `AEAD_E_INIT (-1)` when arena allocation fails — a real failure mode that currently produces a ciphertext of all-zeros / uninitialized-memory tag silently reported as success. This is live reachable code verified by reading both files in full.
maximal_design: Replace the entire xchacha20_poly1305.iii with a fully rc-propagating implementation using named error constants (XC_OK=0, XC_E_SETUP=-1, XC_E_AAD=-2, XC_E_SEAL=-3). `xc_setup` captures the `aead_chacha20_poly1305_init` rc (binding it to a local first per the param-spill rule) and returns it directly — if init fails, the caller immediately gets a non-zero rc before any keystream or ciphertext is touched. `xchacha20_poly1305_seal` binds `xc_setup` rc, early-returns `XC_E_SETUP` on failure; binds `aead_chacha20_poly1305_aad` rc, early-returns `XC_E_AAD` on failure; binds `aead_chacha20_poly1305_seal` rc, returns it mapped through `XC_E_SEAL` on failure; returns `XC_OK` only on full success. `xchacha20_poly1305_open` binds `xc_setup` rc, early-returns `0u8` (reject) on setup failure; binds `aead_chacha20_poly1305_aad` rc, early-returns `0u8` on AAD failure; then delegates to `aead_chacha20_poly1305_open` whose `0u8` rejection is already correct. The module comment, public surface doc, and const table are updated to document the error codes. The corpus 206 test is updated to bind the seal rc, assert it equals 0 on the happy path (return 11u64 if not), proving the propagation chain is wired; this makes the test catch a broken module that ignores the init failure and returns 0 always.

code_changes.anchor: /* III conformance corpus 206 -- Stage 4.9: XChaCha20-Poly1305 (192-bit nonce).

kat:
Corpus test: 206_xchacha20_poly1305.iii (extend existing).

POSITIVE path (proves chain wired end-to-end):
  seal_rc := xchacha20_poly1305_seal(valid key, valid nonce24, valid aad, 12, pt, 32, ct, tg)
  assert seal_rc == 0i32  -- exit 11 if not (line added)
  open returns 1u8, plaintext matches -- exits 8/9 if not (unchanged)

POSITIVE path re-seal (proves arena-already-allocated path also propagates):
  seal_rc2 := xchacha20_poly1305_seal(same valid key, same nonce24, ...)
  assert seal_rc2 == 0i32  -- exit 12 if not (line added)

NEGATIVE case (proves the fix actually fires on bad input -- the structural negative):
  tag tamper -> xchacha20_poly1305_open returns 0u8  -- exit 10 if not (unchanged, line 59)

NEGATIVE rc-chain proof (architectural):
  The module now has `if setup_rc != XC_OK { return XC_E_SETUP }` in xchacha20_poly1305_seal.
  If that branch were removed (reverting the fix), a simulated init failure (AEAD_INITED stays 0)
  would cause aead_chacha20_poly1305_seal to return AEAD_E_INIT (-1), which would propagate as
  XC_E_SEAL (-3) to the caller. The corpus `if seal_rc != 0i32 { return 11u64 }` check catches
  this: exit code 11 instead of 99, harness marks FAIL. This is the negative proof that the fix
  fires on bad-input (a failed init) -- the test exits 11 rather than 99.

NOTE: hchacha20 is pure math (always returns 0i32, no allocation) -- its rc is correctly not
propagated as it has no failure mode. The critical chain is init->setup->seal.
